method_description: Conventional blast-furnace / basic-oxygen-furnace crude steel route using iron ore, coal and some gas, with small scrap input.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian steel-transition studies plus IEA route benchmarks and industry context, with explicit low-confidence caveats for project-specific future routes.
derivation_method: Route archetype synthesis. Per-tonne energy and process emissions are benchmarked to conventional, CCS-enabled, scrap-EAF and H2-DRI route literature, then smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Route intensity is consistent with conventional primary steel benchmarks rather than a plant-specific Australian reconciliation.

method_description: Crude steel from high-scrap electric arc furnace route with modest auxiliary gas use.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian steel-transition studies plus IEA route benchmarks and industry context, with explicit low-confidence caveats for project-specific future routes.
derivation_method: Route archetype synthesis. Per-tonne energy and process emissions are benchmarked to conventional, CCS-enabled, scrap-EAF and H2-DRI route literature, then smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Best-supported low-emissions steel method in Phase 1, but scrap availability limits its maximum national share.

method_description: Primary steel route retaining BF/BOF logic but with substantial capture and storage, lower residual emissions and higher power use.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian steel-transition studies plus IEA route benchmarks and industry context, with explicit low-confidence caveats for project-specific future routes.
derivation_method: Route archetype synthesis. Per-tonne energy and process emissions are benchmarked to conventional, CCS-enabled, scrap-EAF and H2-DRI route literature, then smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Included because CCS remains a plausible transitional primary steel option, but Australian evidence is sparse and route economics are highly project-specific.

method_description: Near-zero primary steel archetype covering hydrogen direct reduced iron with electric melting/finishing (EAF or electric smelting role).
evidence_summary: Built from Australian steel-transition studies plus IEA route benchmarks and industry context, with explicit low-confidence caveats for project-specific future routes.
derivation_method: Route archetype synthesis. Per-tonne energy and process emissions are benchmarked to conventional, CCS-enabled, scrap-EAF and H2-DRI route literature, then smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: This is the main Australian near-zero primary-steel sensitivity method. Costs are especially sensitive to hydrogen and power price assumptions.
